zh.lifeSummary: 當Mabille De Guines - De Marck於1155年出生在法國豪-德-福朗斯帕-德-卡拉斯i斯時，她的父親Comte Baudouin de Guînes II20歲，母親Chrestienne d'Ardres20歲。她於大約1184年與Jan II van Cysoing在法國結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1199年在比利時奧斯吉福拉安德倫沃特格-比特格姆沃特格姆享年44歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Mabille De Guines - De Marck于1155年出生在法国豪-德-福朗斯帕-德-卡拉斯i斯时，她的父亲Comte Baudouin de Guînes II20岁，母亲Chrestienne d'Ardres20岁。她于大约1184年与Jan II van Cysoing在法国结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1199年在比利时奧斯吉福拉安德倫沃特格-比特格姆沃特格姆享年44岁。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Guines
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Perhaps an altered form of Irish Guinness, a shortened form of McGinnis . This surname is found predominantly in TX.

variants.name: McGinnis
meanings: Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mag Aonghuis or Mag Aonghusa ‘son of Aonghus’ (see Angus ). This is the surname of a chieftain family in western County Down. Compare the Scottish form McInnis , and the variant McNeese . Compare also McGuinness .

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Marck
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Flemish, Dutch, and German: variant of   Mark  .
French (Alsace and Lorraine): variant of   Marc   ‘Mark’, or a surname of German origin (see 1 above, compare   Mark   2). This surname is also found in Haiti, where it is rare.

variants.name: Mark
meanings: English, Dutch, and Sorbian: from Latin Marcus, the personal name of Saint Mark the Evangelist, author of the second Gospel in the New Testament. This name was also borne by a number of early Christian saints. Marcus is an ancient Roman name, of uncertain (possibly non-Italic) etymology; it may have some connection with the name of the war god Mars; compare Martin . The personal name was not as popular in England in the Middle Ages as it was elsewhere in Europe, especially in Italy, where the evangelist became the patron of Venice and the Venetian Republic. He was allegedly buried at Aquileia. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates and similar-sounding names from other languages, e.g. Polish, Czech, and Slovak Marek , Ukrainian, Slovenian, Sorbian, Rusyn, and Croatian Marko , Greek Markos , and also their patronymics and other derivatives, e.g. Jewish and Slavic Markowicz and Markovich ; see also 6 below.

English and German; Dutch (Van der Mark): topographic name for someone who lived on a boundary between two districts, from Middle English merke, Middle High German marc, Middle Dutch marke, merke, all meaning ‘borderland’. The German term also denotes an area of fenced-off land (see Marker 5) and, like the English word, is embodied in various placenames which have given rise to habitational names. This surname (in any of the possible senses; see also 5 below) is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine; compare Marck ).
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Marck, Pas-de-Calais, France.

nameMeanings.name: Mabille
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Elaborated form of Mabel , under the influence of the French phrase ma belle ‘my beautiful one’.

en.lifeSummary: When Mabille De Guines - De Marck was born in 1155, in Guînes, Pas-de-Calais, Hauts-de-France, France, her father, Comte Baudouin de Guînes II, was 20 and her mother, Chrestienne d'Ardres, was 20. She married Jan II van Cysoing about 1184, in France. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. She died in 1199, in Wortegem, Wortegem-Petegem, East Flanders, Belgium, at the age of 44.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Mabille De Guines - De Marck est née en 1155, à Guînes, Pas-de-Calais, Hauts-de-France, France, son père, Comte Baudouin de Guînes II, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Chrestienne d'Ardres, avait 20 ans. Elle a épousé Jan II van Cysoing environ 1184, à France. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1199, à Wortegem, Wortegem-Petegem, Flandre-Orientale, Belgique, à l’âge de 44 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Mabille De Guines - De Marck 1155 in Guînes, Pas-de-Calais, Hauts-de-France, Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Comte Baudouin de Guînes II 20 und ihre Mutter Chrestienne d'Ardres 20. Sie hat um 1184 in Frankreich Jan II van Cysoing geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1199 im Alter von 44 Jahren in Wortegem, Wortegem-Petegem, Ostflandern, Belgien.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Mabille De Guines - De Marck è nata il nel 1155 nel Guînes, Pas-de-Calais, Hauts-de-France, Francia, sua padre, Comte Baudouin de Guînes II, aveva 20 anni e sua madre, Chrestienne d'Ardres, aveva 20 anni. Lei sposato Jan II van Cysoing circa 1184, nel Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1199, nel Wortegem, Wortegem-Petegem, Fiandra Orientale, Belgio, all'età di 44 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Mabille De Guines - De Marckが1155年にフランスハウトス-デ-フランスプアス-デ-カレイスグiンスで生まれた時，父Comte Baudouin de Guînes IIは20歳，母Chrestienne d'Ardresは20歳であった。約1184年にフランスでJan II van Cysoingと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1199年にベルギーオオスト-vラアンデレンウオルテジェム-ペテジェムウオルテジェムで44歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Mabille De Guines - De Marck(이)가 1155년 프랑스 하츠-데-프랜스 파스-데-캘레이 구i네스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Comte Baudouin de Guînes II(은)는 20세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Chrestienne d'Ardres(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 대략 1184년 프랑스에서 Jan II van Cysoing(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1199년 벨기에 스트-블라아더 워트그-피테그 워트그에서 44세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Mabille De Guines - De Marck nasceu em 1155, em Guînes, Pas-de-Calais, Hauts-de-France, França, seu pai, Comte Baudouin de Guînes II, tinha 20 anos e sua mãe, Chrestienne d'Ardres, tinha 20 anos. Ela casou-se com Jan II van Cysoing aproximadamente 1184, em França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1199, em Wortegem, Wortegem-Petegem, Flandres Oriental, Bélgica, com 44 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Mabille De Guines - De Marck родилась в 1155 г. в местности гуiнесь, пас-де-калеи, хауц-де-фрэнс, Франция, её отцу по имени Comte Baudouin de Guînes II было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Chrestienne d'Ardres, 20. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Jan II van Cysoing около 1184 г. в местности Франция. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1199 г. в местности вортегем, вортегем-питегем, уст-влаандирен, Бельгия в возрасте 44 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Mabille De Guines - De Marck nació en 1155, en Guînes, Pas-de-Calais, Hauts-de-France, Francia, su papá, Comte Baudouin de Guînes II, tenía 20 años y su mamá, Chrestienne d'Ardres, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Jan II van Cysoing aproximadamente 1184, en Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en 1199, en Wortegem, Wortegem-Petegem, Flandes Oriental, Bélgica, a la edad de 44 años.
